chief_complaint: A 7-year-old intact female mixed-breed dog presented with severe anorexia and lethargy. The dog had a 4-week history of polyuria, polydipsia, polyphagia, and weight loss.
history: The dog had a 4-week history of polyuria, polydipsia, polyphagia, and weight loss. No past medical history or current medications were reported.
exam: Severe dehydration, heart rate of 120 beats/min, respiratory rate of 24 breaths/min, and rectal temperature of 37.5 degrees C.
labs: ECG showed regular sinus rhythm, negative QRS complexes in multiple leads, and an unusually shifted mean electrical axis at -101 degrees. Serum cardiac troponin I levels were elevated (0.5 ng/mL). Emergency echocardiography revealed no abnormalities. Laboratory tests confirmed diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) characterised by severe hyperglycaemia, ketonemia, and metabolic acidosis with an increased anion gap.
correct_diagnosis: Diabetic Ketoacidosis with Extreme Axis Deviation
differentials: Diabetic Ketoacidosis with Extreme Axis Deviation
correct_ranking: Diabetic Ketoacidosis with Extreme Axis Deviation
